event: Study of an apostle's head and architectural sketch
symbols: Apostle's head gazing downward — the contemplative posture of a disciple at the Last Supper or similar scene
Church architecture — the building of the Church that the apostles established
description: A red chalk drawing on paper combines two studies. The upper portion shows the head and shoulders of a young man with curly hair, gazing downward with a pensive expression, drawn in delicate, precise strokes. The lower left features an architectural sketch of a domed church or centralized building with multiple cupolas and a cross-topped lantern. The number '44' appears written to the right. A small stamp mark is visible at the bottom.
theological_description: This preparatory study likely relates to a Last Supper or similar apostolic scene. The apostles were the foundation stones of the Church — 'built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the cornerstone' (EPH.2.20). The juxtaposition of the apostle's head with church architecture on the same sheet is a suggestive visual theology: the apostles and the churches they built are intimately connected. The downward gaze suggests the moment of sorrow or self-examination at the Last Supper when Jesus announced 'one of you will betray me' and each disciple asked 'Is it I, Lord?' (MAT.26.21-22).

scripture_refs.reason: The apostle's pensive downward gaze may relate to the moment of self-examination at the Last Supper
Built on the foundation of the apostles — the connection between apostle and church architecture
